meaning: adj.使眩目的,使失去判断力的n.碎石子
examples: The sun hit the windshield, momentarily blinding him.
There was a blinding flash and the whole building shuddered.
The doctor worked busily beneath the blinding lights of the delivery room.
"Venice: Under the Rialto Bridge" (1909) is almost equal parts dark shadow and blinding light.
The problem with the phrase is that is the blinding flash of the obvious.
